ety: prob. fr. S. कण्टक; cf. kaṇṭār
def: v.n. To bristle (at, -se); to be averse (to), to dislike, abhor.

ety: prob. kand+sār, qq.v. cf. also S. कृष्णसार
def: s.m. A stag, a buck, a deer.
s.m. A caste whose business it is to make sweetmeats, fry or parch grain, &c.; an individual of that caste, a sugar-boiler; a grain-parcher (syn. bhaṛbhūṅjā).
s.f. Scratching itching; the itch.
s.m. A kind of coarse grain.
s.m. A ball (to play with; syn. geṅḍ); — a pillow.
s.m. (dialec.), prob. corr. of kātar, q.v.

s.f. A leathern (or linen) cover for a table; a napkin; — a feast held in honour of Fātima, of which only women (and those the most virtuous) can partake (no male is ever permitted to see the food dressed on this occasion and set apart for an offering to the prophet's daughter)

ety: act. part. of قنع 'to be contented,' &c.; see qanāʻat
def: adj. Contented, satisfied (with, -par).

ety: Prk. कणे, Ap. Prk. कणिहिं; S. कर्णे, loc. of कर्ण
def: postpn. By the side (of, -ke), near (to), in proximity (to), close (by); to; with, in the possession (of); at; at the house (of)
s.m. = kaner, q.v.
postpn. (dialec.) = kane, q.v.
s.m. Fragrant oleander or rose-bay, Nerium odorum (the bark and root are poisonous; the leaves are used in native medicine).

r: kaneṭī, kān, eṭī, aiṅṭnī, aiṅṭhnī, aiṅṭhnā
ety: kān+eṭī = aiṅṭnī = aiṅṭhnī, fem. of aiṅṭhnā, q.v.
def: s.f. Twisting the ear, pulling the ear.
s.f. (dialec.) = kaṅghī, q.v.
adj. Poor, indigent, miserable, wretched, friendless; beggarly, mean; miserly
subs: adj. & s.m. Poor and vain or conceited; — a penniless swell
adj. & s.m. Poor and vain or insolent; vainglorious; — a vainglorious man
v.n. To become poor, to be reduced to poverty.
wety: Borrowed from Northwestern Indo-Aryan (whence Punjabi کنگال (kaṅgāl)), from Prakrit 𑀓𑀁𑀓𑀸𑀮 (kaṃkāla), from Sanskrit कङ्काल (kaṅkāla, “skeleton”). Cognate with Gujarati કંગાલ (kaṅgāl), Bengali কাঙাল (kaṅal), Assamese কঙাল (koṅal), and possibly Marathi कंकाळ (kaṅkāḷ, “cruel”).

def: adj. & s.m. Poor, indigent, &c.; — a pauper, a beggar
adj. & s.m. Poor, indigent, &c. ( = kaṅgāl, q.v.); — a poor man, a pauper; — s.f. Poverty, &c. ( = kaṅgāltā, q.v.)
subs: (-ko), To be or become poverty-stricken, be reduced to poverty, &c.

def: contrac. of kaṅgālī, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = kaṅgālī, q.v.; — s.f. = kaṅkālin, q.v.

ety: Prk. कंकालप्पणं; S. कङ्काल + त्वं (Vedic त्वनं); and ता
def: Poverty, indigence, penury, destitution, wretcheduess, miserable state or condition.

ety: Prk. कंकालप्पणं; S. कङ्काल + त्वं (Vedic त्वनं); and ता
def: Poverty, indigence, penury, destitution, wretcheduess, miserable state or condition.
s.f. contrac. of kaṅgāltā, q.v.
s.m. A gold or silver ornament for the wrist (worn by women), a bracelet.
wety: Borrowed from Punjabi کنگن (kngn), from Prakrit 𑀓𑀁𑀓𑀡 (kaṃkaṇa), from Sanskrit कङ्कण (kaṅkaṇa).

def: A kind of panic seed, Panicum italicum; millet ( = kāṅgnī).
s.m. A comb, a hair-comb (such as is used by a man; it has teeth on one side only, and a curved back, and is fixed in the top-knot).
s.f. A comb, a hair-comb (such as is used by women; it generally has teeth on both sides); a tooth-comb; — the tree Sida populifolia, or Sida indica (the fruit of which resembles a comb)
subs: To braid or dress the hair
To adjust or tidy the hair (after sleeping, &c.)

def: s.m. (Dakkhanī), A kind of rake or harrow.
s.f. (Dakkhanī), A large jar or vessel in which grain is kept (it is usually made of split bamboo or wicker-work plastered with clay).

s.m. A bracelet; — a thread or string tied round the right wrist of a bridegroom and the left of a bride at the marriage ceremony; — kaṅgnā or kaṅgan kholnā, The marriage ceremony of untying the kaṅgnā (the bride untying the bridegroom's, and vice versâ).
s.f. A small bracelet, or ornament for the wrist; — a prominent band (round a turban, &c.); a cordon; an ornamental and projecting line along a wall, or around a column, &c.; a cornice (cf. kangura)
subs: adj. Having an ornamental band, &c.; having a cornice.

def: s.f. A kind of small grain, millet, Panicum italicum.
A kind of panic seed, Panicum italicum; millet ( = kāṅgnī).
s.f. A small portable fire-place, a chafing dish, brasier.
s.m. Ridge of the back, back-bone, spine (cf. kaṅkāl); — a species of water-fowl.
s.f. (dialec.) = kaṅghī, q.v. (and cf. kaṅgwā).
s.m. = کنگره kangura, q.v.
s.m. = کنگره kangura, q.v.
s.f. The outer and curved part of the ear; the lobe of the ear.
s.m. (dialec.) = kaṅghā, q.v.
s.m. A name of Krishṇa
subs: or kāṅh-pūr, s.m. The town of Kāṅhpūr or Cawnpore.

def: s.m. A husband ( = kān and kānta, q.v.).
s.f. Valuation of a standing crop (syn. kan-kūt).
s.m. = kanhaiyā, q.v.
s.m. = kanhaiyā.

ety: Prk. कण्हयओ, or कण्ह ययो; S. कृष्णक+कः
def: s.m. A name of Krishṇa; — a proper name.
s.m. A name of Krishṇa ( = kāṅh, q.v.).
adj. Listening attentively, attentive; quick of hearing; acute (cf. kanharā).
s.m. Rudder, helm.
s.m. Listening, attention.

ety: kanhar+S. इल+कः = H کنهريا कनहरिया kanhariyā [kanhar+S. aff. इकः
def: s.m. Helmsman, stearsman.
s.m. Helmsman, stearsman.
s.m. Name of a rāginī or musical mode; — a kind of nightingale.
s.f. The ear ( = kān).
s.f. Resentment, enmity, rancour, spite (syn. kīna).
adj. Of or relating to a mine; obtained from a mine; mineral; fossil
s.f. A woman blind of one eye.

s.f. A particle, an atom; a fragment, piece; a grain, or broken piece (of rice, &c.); bran; a small piece of crystal or diamond; spark of a diamond or other gem; a particle of strength or power; a hard or uncooked grain (of rice, &c.); — a bulbous root
subs: (-kī), To effect the ruin (of a person) by clandestine means, to undermine (a person).

def: s.m. (f. -ī), Spiteful, malicious, vindictive, revengeful, rancorous, malevolent; — a spiteful person, &c.
s.m. Wheat; wheaten flour; flour; meal.
s.f. A small particle, an atom; a granule; a grain (of corn, &c.); fine sand (particularly grittiness of sand in bread, &c.); — a kind of rice.
s.m. & adv. (dialec.) A particle, a very little, the least bit (cf. kaṇ).
s.m. Name of a caste of people (generally arrow-makers).
s.f. The little finger; — younger sister.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Smallest, least; very small; little; — youngest, younger born; junior; — subordinate, sub-; younger brother, &c.
s.f. The little finger.

ety: act. part. of قنت 'to be obedient' (to God
def: adj. Obedient to God, devout, godly, prayerful; — silent.
s.m. = kanahā, q.v.
s.f. Shrinking; skulking; avoiding, shunning; shyness, reserve; bashfulness, coyness; timidity.
s.f. The lap.

ety: fr. kannī ( = S. कर्णिका)+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि (caus. augment)+nā
def: v.n. To move aside or apart (from, -se), to shrink (from), to skulk away (from), to feel shy or ashamed (of), to avoid, shun; to incline or lean to one side (as a paper kite; see kannī khānā).

ety: Pārsī & Pehl. kanīk; Zend kainika; S. कन्यका; or Zend kanya ( = S. कन्या) +dim. aff. īza or za = ća = S. ka
def: s.f. A maid-servant, hand-maid, slave-girl
subs: (f. -ī), Son of a slave-girl or bond-woman.

ety: Pārsī & Pehl. kanīk; Zend kainika; S. कन्यका; or Zend kanya ( = S. कन्या) +dim. aff. īza or za = ća = S. ka
def: s.f. A maid-servant, hand-maid, slave-girl
subs: (f. -ī), Son of a slave-girl or bond-woman.

ety: kanīz, q.v.+ī = Zend i = S. इ
def: s.f. State of a maid-servant or slave-girl, the being a maid-servant, &c.; servitude.
s.m. Hair (of the head); — an epithet of Brahmā; — a lotus (lit. 'water-born'); — the food of the immortals (syn. amr̤it).
adj. (f. -ī), Blue-eyed; grey-eyed.
s.m.(?) (Dakkhinī) The green scum formed on stagnant water.

s.m. A class of people of low caste whose employment consists in making and selling strings, &c. (they also catch and eat snakes; and are credited with eating carcases); a man of that class; — a class of gipsies; — (met.) a dirty, low fellow
s.f. A woman of the kanjar caste
subs: (in the Panjāb) a prostitute; — a class of songs sung by women of the Kanjar caste.

def: s.f. A woman of the kanjar caste
subs: (in the Panjāb) a prostitute; — a class of songs sung by women of the Kanjar caste.

def: s.f. A kind of sour gruel, or vinegar, made by steeping rice, or mustard-seed, &c. in water and letting the liquor ferment (it is said to create appetite and aid digestion); — water in which rice has been boiled, rice-gruel, 'congee'; — starch (prepared from water in which rice has been boiled)
subs: s.m. (colloq.) 'Congee-house,' a lock-up; a pound (for cattle).

def: s.f. A stye (in the eye).
s.m. = kājū, q.v.
s.m. A miser, niggard, skin-flint, curmudgeon.
s.f. = H کنجوسپنا कंजूसपना kanjūs-panā, s.m. Miserliness, niggardliness, stinginess, meanness, penuriousness.
s.m. = H کنجوسي कंजूसी kanjūsī, s.f. Miserliness, niggardliness, stinginess, meanness, penuriousness.
s.m. (f. -ā), A heron; — an epithet of Yama; a false or pretended Brāhman; one of the eighteen divisions of the continent of India; — the back of a sword.
adj. Of or relating to Kankā (a place famous for its breed of horses).
s.f. dim. of kan-kauwā, q.v.
s.m. A skeleton; — the back-bone; — the loins, waist.
s.f. (rustic), A sort of stiff sandy or gravelly loam.
s.f. A witch, sorceress, hag.
s.f. Cawing (of a crow, &c.); quacking, quack (of a duck)
s.f. See s.v. kāṅ.
s.m. An ornament for the wrist; a bracelet (see kaṅgan); — a kind of weapon (or the wrist-ornament used as such).
s.m. = kankaṇ.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Ornamented with (or wearing) a bracelet; — one who wears a bracelet, one who has a bracelet on.
s.m. (prov.) = kankar, q.v.
s.m. A nodule of limestone, limestone (of a coarse kind found in many parts of India, and used in making lime, roads, &c.); stone, gravel, pebble
subs: s.m. Pebbles and stones; (met.) rubbish, trash
adj. (f. -ī), Made of limestone; metalled (as a road)
A gugglet made of kaṅkar (in which water keeps cool)
To lay down kaṅkar on a road, to metal a road.

def: s.m. A nodule or piece of kaṅkar; a stone ball (with which boys play), a marble; a pebble, stone
subs: The disease of the stone or gravel.

def: s.f. A nodule, a small piece of kaṅkar; a small round stone or pebble; gravel; a small lump (of sugar, or salt, &c.).
s.m. = S کنکتي कंकती kaṅkatī, s.f. A comb, hair-comb.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Ear-cropt, earless; — a person whose ears have been cut off; — (for kān-kāṭā) one who cuts off the ears (of another; syn. kān-kāṭne-wālā).
s.f. A disorder in the ears so called.
s.f. = S کنکت कंकत kaṅkat, s.m. A comb, hair-comb.
s.m. A square paper kite (which is flown with silk-line, called nak); — a kind of wild herb, Ipomæa muricata
subs: To set one kite against another, to endeavour to cut the silk-line of a (rival) kite (by entangling the line of one's own kite with it)
s.m. One who is addicted to kite-flying.

def: s.f. Armpit; side, flank (syn. bag̠al).
s.m. Violent effort, straining (as in lifting a heavy load, or at stool, &c.); grunting, grunt; groan, moan; snort; snore.
s.m. A centipede, Scolopendra.
v.n. To strain; to grunt; to groan, moan; to snort; to snore.
s.m. (dialec.) = kākṛā, q.v.
adj. = next, q.v.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Abounding in kaṅkar; stony; gravelly; gritty; — stony or gravelly soil.
s.m. A kind of gourd, Momordica miæta.
s.f. Desire, inclination, wish, longing.
adj. & s.m. (f. -iṉī), Desiring, expecting, longing for; — a desirer, an expectant.
part. adj. (f. -ā), Wished, desired, expected, longed for; desirous, longing.
s.f. Wish, desire.
s.m. = kambu-grīv, q.v.
s.f. & m. See s.v. kaṇ or kan (and cf. kūt).

s.m. Edge, rim; projection or handle (on the side of a vessel, &c., or one of two handles on the sides); one of the notches on the two sides of the upper leather of a shoe; — the part of a paper kite to which the string is tied
subs: vulg. kanne-dār, s.m. A shoe or slipper with a notch at the side, or with a projecting piece at the heel (to pull it on by)
(-kā), The rim or edge, or the handle (of a vessel), to be broken
(The string of a paper kite) to come clean out from, or be cut off at, the parts where it is tied; (met.) to fly off in a dudgeon, to turn away in a huff.

ety: A. قنارة qinnārat; prob. an arabicized word
def: s.m. A slaughter-house; shambles; — a row of iron hooks on which butcher's meat is suspended; a butcher's shop.

ety: fr. kans, 'sweeping,' inf. n. of کنس
def: s.m. A sweeper; (met.) an avaricious man, a miser.
s.f. The little finger.
s.f. Outer edge, border, hem (see kannā); — a fillet, or a make-weight, attached to the lighter side of a paper-kite
subs: (-meṅ), To tie a fillet as a make-weight to the lighter side of a paper-kite
adj. Having a border or hem
(-kī), The power (of a person) to submit (to, -se, that of another)
To lean over, to incline to one side (a paper kite).

def: s.f. (dialec.), The ear of a horse.
s.f. The bow (of a paper kite); a slender waist; — tusk (of a boar).
s.m. (dialec.) Cutting; a cut, slice, piece, bit (of fruit, &c.); an ear-ornament (of women); a deposit of mud (formed by a river or water-course); — a stroke of adverse fortune, misfortune, ill-luck
s.f. The temple; the upper part of the side of the face (between the temple and the ear)
s.m. = kāʼephal, q.v.
s.m. One whose ears are slit; — a kind of Hindū faqīr, with slit ears.
s.m. One whose ears are slit; — a kind of Hindū faqīr, with slit ears.
s.m. Swelling of the glands between the ear and the throat.
s.m. One who blows or breathes into the ear (of another); any religious teacher (among the Hindūs) who whispers his instructions in the ears of his followers.

ety: Prk. कंपणअं; S. कम्पनीयं, rt. कम्प्
def: v.n. To tremble, shake, quake, quiver, shiver
subs: v.n. To break into trembling, to begin to tremble, or shake, &c.
ex: وہ اتنا خوفزدہ ہو چکا تھا کہ وہ کانپنے سے رک نہیں سکا۔
He became so scared that he could not stop trembling.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀓𑀁𑀧𑀇 (kaṃpaï), from Sanskrit कम्पते (kampate). Cognates include Gujarati કંપવું (kampvũ), Marwari कांपणौ (kā̃mpṇau), Assamese কঁপা (kõpa), Bengali কাঁপা (kãpa).

def: adj. & s.m. = kānā, q.v.
s.m. Taking pleasure in hearing (music, &c.); the pleasure afforded to the ear (by music, &c.).
s.m. One who takes pleasure in listening to music, a lover of music; an amateur in music.
s.f. A rafter, beam; yoke (of a vehicle, or a plough); — ulceration on the soles of the feet of an elephant ( = kāṅḍī).
s.f. Name of a musical mode or rāginī, and of a measure in versification.

ety: kāṅṛ˚ = Prk. कंड इ) or कंडे(इ) = S. कण्डय(ति), rt. कण्ड्
def: v.t. To tread on, trample; to crush.
s.f. A tall species of grass that grows luxuriantly on waste and sandy soils, Imperata spontanea; — also = kās, q.v.
subs: 'To swim in the long grass called kāṅs'; to move swimmingly along in fancy; to soar on the wings of fancy; to be deluded by a mirage.

def: s.m. A Rājā of Mathura (he was the cousin and implacable enemy of Krishṇa, by whom he was slain; as the foe of the deity, he is identified with the Asur Kālanemī).

ety: Prk. कंसओ; S. कंस or कांस्य +कः
def: s.m. Bell-metal; any amalgam of zinc and copper; white copper; queen's metal; brass; — a drinking vessel (of bell-metal, or brass, &c.), a cup, goblet, bowl; a large flat dish
subs: s.m. A brasier, a maker of cups, goblets, &c.

ety: Prk. कंसओ; S. कंस or कांस्य +कः
def: s.m. Bell-metal; any amalgam of zinc and copper; white copper; queen's metal; brass; — a drinking vessel (of bell-metal, or brass, &c.), a cup, goblet, bowl; a large flat dish
subs: s.m. A brasier, a maker of cups, goblets, &c.

def: s.f. A kind of scolopendra or julus (said to get into the brain through the ears, and by its bite to occasion excessive pain and even death).
s.f. = kan-salāʼī, q.v.
s.f. Bell-metal, &c. ( = kāṅsā).
s.f. Hearing, listening, eavesdropping
s.m. A worm that destroys or injures young sugar-cane.
adj. Consisting of white copper, &c.; made of brass; — s.m. Bell-metal; brass, &c. ( = kāṅsā); a metallic vessel or cup
subs: s.m. A worker in bell-metal; a brasier, a pewterer
s.m. Blue vitriol (considered as a collyrium).

def: s.m. One beloved, a husband; a lover, a sweetheart
subs: s.m. The being a husband, the state or condition of husband; the disposition and sentiments appropriate in a woman towards her beloved.

def: s.f. A beloved or lovely woman; a wife, a sweetheart, mistress; — loveliness, beauty, &c. ( = kānti).

s.m. A thorn, prickle, spine; needle or pricker (of a gun, &c.); a nail (syn. kīl); sting (of a scorpion, &c.); spur (of a cock, &c.), rowel (of a spur); bone (of a fish); quill (of a porcupine); hand (of a watch or clock); a fork (e.g. ćhuṛī-kāṅṭā, 'knife and fork'); a prong; a pitchfork; a type; tongue (of a balance, or a buckle, &c.); bolt (of a lock); a balance having a tongue, small scales (esp. such as are used by goldsmiths); a hook (to hang a thing on; and as in 'hook and eye'); a fish-hook; a hooked instrument (for pulling buckets out of water, &c.); (fig.) a thorn in the side, a hateful person, a troublesome fellow, a pest, plague; erection of the hairs of the body (from joy, fear, &c.), horripilation; dryness, or roughness, or eruption (on the tongue, or the body, after fever, &c.); a disease in the throat of birds; (in Arith.) a cross drawn to prove a multiplication sum; proving, proof (of a sum); — a kind of fire-work (a large hollow species of thorn filled with powder); — viscosity, stickiness, adhesiveness (e.g. ćāshnī-kā kāṅṭā); congelation, or crystal (of candy, &c.)
subs: (-meṅ), To let down a hook or a hooked instrument (into a well, &c. in order to fish up anything)
v.n. To prick as a thorn; to rankle (in)
v.n. 'To be taken out as a thorn'; to be freed from distress, or pain, or injury
(-kā), To prove (a sum) by means of a cross (kāṅṭā)
(-ko), To strike with its prickles or fins (a fish)
s.m. A clerk who notes and registers weighments
(-kā), To extricate (one's) thorn (from, -se); to afford relief (to one in pain)
v.n. 'To become like a thorn,' to become lean, become as thin as a broom-stick
v.n. 'To plant thorns'; to prepare distress or misfortune for oneself; to sow troubles or dissensions
adj. Thorny, prickly; bristly; (in Bot.) mucronate; hispid
(or -meṅ) ghasīṭnā (-ko), To drag upon (or among) thorns; to distress (one) by excessive attentions or compliments, or by unmerited or undue eulogy, &c.
(or -meṅ) loṭnā, To roll on (or in) thorns; to be in great pain or distress, &c.
s.m. (local) The urchin or hedgehog, Erinaceus setosus

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Beloved; lovely, beautiful, pleasing, agreeable; — a lover; a husband.
adj. Happy
s.m. A thorn; a sharp bone; a fish-bone; a stinging pain; a vile, hateful fellow; a mean, sordid fellow, a miser; a mean enemy; an ill-natured man; a merciless brute; a wicked or bad man.
s.f. A sort of prickly nightshade, Solanum jacquini; — the silk-cotton tree, Bombax heptaphyllum (and several other prickly plants).
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Thorny, prickly; vexatious, annoying; — a vexatious person, &c.
adj. Thorny, prickly.
adj. & s.m. Miserly, niggardly; — a miser, &c. (see kanṭak).
s.m. (dialec. or rustic) = H. kant, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), Near, proximate, close at hand, in the immediate vicinity (of).
s.m. (Dakkhanī), corr. of kathā, q.v.
s.m. A gold necklet; a necklace or rosary (of beads of gold, or silver, or pearls, or crystal, or the earth of Karbalā); a necklace of flowers; — embroidery for the neck
s.f.(m.?) A patched cloth or garment, a covering of rags and patches (such as is worn by jogīs and faqīrs; syn. gudṛī); a beggar's wallet and band; — a wall.
s.m. (dialec. or rustic) = H. kant, q.v.
s.m. The neck; the throat, windpipe, larynx; Adam's apple; the voice; the bosom; — immediate proximity; — adj. & adv. Committed to memory; — ready to be repeated; — by heart, by rote
subs: (˚ṭha+ag˚), s.m. The top or beginning of the throat; — adv. In the mouth ready to be repeated; — by heart, by rote
s.m. A guttural letter (e.g. k, kh, &c.)
s.f. A short necklace, a collar
The throat to become hoarse, the voice to set
The voice to crack or break (as that of a boy approaching puberty)
adj. (in Gram.) Gutturo-palatal (letters, e.g. ए e, ऐ ai)
adj. & adv. Situate in the throat; guttural; ready on the tongue, ready to be repeated; by heart, by rote
v.t. lit. 'To get or have (a lesson, &c.) on the tongue ready to be repeated'; to get by heart, to commit to memory
s.f. A kind of necklace of gold and jewels; — the bronchocele or goitre; scrofula
s.m. A jewel worn on the throat
'Adam's apple to come out'; to arrive at puberty
(˚ṭha+osh˚), s.m. (in Gram.) A gutturo-labial letter (e.g. ओ o, औ au).

def: s.f. A short necklace formed of beads of gold, &c. (see kaṇṭhā).

ety: kaṇṭhā, q.v.+lā = Prk. डओ = S. र or ल+कः
def: s.m. A necklace formed of pieces of gold, silver, &c. (put on children to avert evil; — syn. kaṇṭh-mālā; kaṇṭhī).
adj. & s.m. Belonging to the throat, guttural; — a guttural letter.
s.f. Loveliness, beauty; personal decoration or embellishment; splendour, lustre; gloss.
s.f. A little thorn, a prickle; a thorny tree or bush; a prickly hedge; — a small hook; a small pair of scales, &c.; — refuse of cotton.
adj. (f. -ī), Thorny, prickly.
s.f. A hook; a fish-hook; — a vessel for keeping oil.
s.m. A cap covering the ears.

ety: P. also qānūn; akin to Gr. κανὼν
def: s.m. A canon, rule, regulation, law, legislative act, statute, ordinance; — a species of dulcimer or harp
subs: s.m. One who is versed or learned in the law, a lawyer, jurist
s.f. Knowledge of the law; — jurisprudence

s.m. An officer in each district, acquainted with its customs, the nature of the tenure of the lands, &c.; registrar of a pargana, or the hereditary registrar of landed property in a pargana; a superintendent of village accountants
s.f. The office of a qanūn-go.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قَانُون (qānūn), borrowed from Arabic قَانُون (qānūn), borrowed from Ancient Greek κᾰνών (kănṓn). First attested in c. 1564 as Middle Hindi قانون (qanvn /⁠qānūn⁠/).

def: adv. By law, according to law, legally.

adj. Legal, according to law, constitutional; legislative; having the sanction or authority of law; — controversial; litigious; — qānūniyā, s.m. One versed in the law, &c. ( = qānūn-dān); — a controversialist; a litigious person.

adj. Legal, according to law, constitutional; legislative; having the sanction or authority of law; — controversial; litigious; — qānūniyā, s.m. One versed in the law, &c. ( = qānūn-dān); — a controversialist; a litigious person.
s.m. A measure of capacity, the twentieth paṛt of a seer (ser).
s.f. Boring or piercing the ears.
s.f. A kind of clayey soil.

ety: Prk. कवँलु and कमलं; S. कमलं
def: s.m. The lotus, Nymphæa nelumbo, or N. alba; — (met.) the heart; — the shade of mica and coloured paper, representing the lotus (within which wax candles are burnt and floated on the water on festivals, especially on Thursday in the month of Bhādoṅ, as an oblation to Khẉāja Khiẓr; see s.v. beṛā); a glass shade
subs: s.m. Lotus-feet, an epithet of the feet of any deity; kaṅwal-dah, s.m. Lotus-pool, very deep water abounding with lotuses
s.m. The edible seed of the lotus
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi कवल (kavala), from Apabhramsa कमल (kamala), from Prakrit 𑀓𑀫𑀮 (kamala), from Sanskrit कमल (kamala). Doublet of کَمَل (kamal).

def: s.f. The jaundice
subs: or kaṅwal-bāʼī, or kaṅwal-bāʼo, s.f. Idem.
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi कवल (kavala), from Apabhramsa कमल (kamala), from Prakrit 𑀓𑀫𑀮 (kamala), from Sanskrit कमल (kamala). Doublet of کَمَل (kamal).

def: s.m. (dialec.) = kiwāṅć, q.v.

ety: prob. Prk. कमढो; S. कमठः 'a bamboo'
def: s.f. A bamboo lath with slings at each end (for the conveyance across the shoulder of pitchers, baskets, &c.; — syn. bahangī); a bamboo or pole with baskets slung at each end in which water from a holy river is carried
s.f. = kāṅwar, q.v.
s.m. A boil on the temple.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀓𑀼𑀫𑀸𑀭 (kumāra), from Sanskrit कुमार (kumārá). Doublet of کمار (kumār).

def: adj. In a state of excitement, excited, wild, frenzied; restless, impatient

ety: kāṅwar, q.v.+S. aff. इकः; but cf. S. कार्पटिकः, and H. kāmārthī
def: s.m. One who carries (baskets, &c.) by means of a kāṅwar; a pilgrim who carries water from sacred rivers.
s.f. Cawing (of a crow); noise, clamour
corr. of kām-rūp, q.v.s.v. kām.
s.f. A girl (not above ten years of age), an unmarried girl, a maiden, a virgin; a daughter; the sign Virgo; — a name of Durgā
subs: (The bridegroom's) taking the hand of the bride
or kanyā-sut, s.m. The son of an unmarried girl
s.m. The giving a girl in marriage; — a dowry

s.m. The ancient name of the city and country of Kanauj (the etymology — kanyā, 'girl,' and kubja, 'crooked' — refers to a legend relating to the hundred daughters of Kuśanābha, the king of the city, who were all rendered crooked by Vāyu for non-compliance with his licentious desires)
s.f. 'The youthful goddess,' an epithet of Durgā; — name of the southern promontory of the peninsula of India, Cape Comorin
s.f. Rain that falls when the sun is in Virgo, September rain.

def: s.f. A girl, maiden ( = kanyā); — the Socotrine aloe, Aloe perfoliata.
s.m. The ancient name of the country of Kanauj.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Smaller, younger, junior; — a younger brother.
s.m. Maidenhood, virginity ( = kuʼār-patī).
s.m. Treasuring, hoarding; a treasure, store.
s.f. = kāṅw-kāṅw, and kāʼeṅ-kāʼeṅ, qq.v.
s.m. Tremor, trembling ( = kaṅp)
subs: s.f. Tremor, trembling, shivering, quaking; ague; perturbation
(-ko), A fit of shivering or ague to come on.

def: s.m. The skull, the cranium, the skull-bone; the head; the forehead; the glene; (met.) fate, destiny; — the fragment of a vessel, a potsherd; either half of a water-jar; the shell (of an egg, &c.); — a kind of leprosy
subs: (-kā), 'The head to be broken'; to be unfortunate; — kapāl-kriyā, s.f. The ceremony of breaking the skull of a corpse (when a dead body is burning, and nearly reduced to ashes, the son or the nearest relation breaks the skull with the stroke of a bamboo and pours melted butter into the cavity)
(-kī, or apnī), To break the skull of a burnt corpse with a bamboo, &c.; (met.) to beat or cudgel one's brains, to think intensely
(-kā), To have a favourable turn of fortune.

def: adj. & s.m. Wearing skulls as a necklace; covered with skulls; an epithet of Śiva (who carries a skull in his hand, and wears a chain of skulls round his neck); — long-headed, clever, shrewd, cunning, sly; — a long-headed or shrewd person, &c.
subs: s.m. Pain of half the head, hemicrania; — an areca nut having two kernels, or rather, one of those kernels which are convex on the one side and flat on the other, where it was in contact with its fellow (these nuts, from the doctrine of signatures, are said to be a remedy for hemicrania)
s.m. One of the attitudes of faqīrs in worship, in which they stand on their heads

def: s.m. & s.f. = kapārī, q.v.
s.f. A cavesson or nose-band for a horse.
s.m. = kapāl, q.v.
s.m. (dialec.), See kapaṛ and kapṛā.

ety: Prk. कप्पडो, and कप्पडओ; S. कर्पटः and कर्पट+कः
def: s.m. Cloth; clothing, clothes, dress, habit
subs: or kapṛoṅ-kī pot, s.f. A bundle of clothes
or kapaṛ-dhūl, s.m. Tissue; gauze; crape (worn as a sāṛī); a silk cloth wrought with gold and silver flowers (worn as a head-dress by women)
or kapaṛ-ćhan, adj. Thoroughly strained or sifted; impalpable (powder); deep (consultation)
(or -ćhan) karnā, v.t. To strain, to sift
(S. karpaṭa+kuṭta), s.m. lit. 'Pounded up with rags'; clay made for crucibles, &c.
or kapṛā pahannā, To put on a covering or a garment, to cover or clothe oneself
s.m. Clothes, clothing, articles of apparel
s.m. pl. Clothes, clothing, dress, apparel
(-ko), or kapṛoṅ-se honā, or kapṛoṅ honā, v.n. To have the menses
To put on clothes, to dress
To dye cloth or clothes; to become a faqīr
To give an alarm (by making the clothes bulge out)

def: s.f. A skull ( = kapāl).
adj. & s.m. = kapālī, q.v.
s.m. A caste (in Bengal) who sell greens, &c.
s.f. The cotton-plant, Gosypium herbaceum; cotton (not separated from the seed).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀓𑀧𑁆𑀧𑀸𑀲 (kappāsa), from Sanskrit कर्पास (karpāsa, “cotton”), ultimately from Munda. Possibly related to Sanskrit कर्पट (karpaṭa), whence Urdu کَپْڑَا (kapṛā, “cloth, garment”).

def: adj. Made of or from cotton, cotton (stuff); cottony, downy; of the colour of the cotton plant, or its flower, light green, yellow.
s.m. A door; shutter; panel (of a door); leaf (of a door).
adj. False, insincere, &c. ( = kapaṭī, q.v.)
subs: s.f. 'This world of deceit, &c.,' the present world
s.m. One who deceitfully pretends to be an ascetic, a false or feigned ascetic
(-se), To dissimulate; to harbour malice, or evil designs (against)
(-ke sāth), To dissimulate, to pretend; to practise deceit (on), to deceive, cheat
s.m. Disguise; an assumed character; hypocrisy
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Disguised, in masquerade; hypocritical; — a person in disguise; a hypocrite.

def: s.f. = S کپٿتو कपटत्व kapaṭatva, s.m. Dissimulation, hypocrisy, falseness, deceitfulness.
s.m. = S کپٿتا कपटता kapaṭatā, s.f. Dissimulation, hypocrisy, falseness, deceitfulness.
s.m. A wrong road; a bad course ( = ku-path).

adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Insincere, false, hypocritical; designing, dishonest, fraudulent, deceitful; evil-minded, ill-disposed, malicious; false, not genuine, adulterated; — a dissembler, hypocrite, deceiver, fraudulent man, cheat, rogue, &c.
s.f. A snaffle-bridle.
adj. & s.m. = next, q.v.
s.m. Wickedness, vice; dissimulation; dishonesty, fraud.
s.m. (dialec.), A kind of jacket.
s.m. Phleghm; spittle; scum, foam, froth.
s.m. A bitter seed used in medicine ( = kāʼe-phal and kaṭ-phal, qq.v.).
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Phlegmatic, filled with mucus, afflicted with rheum; one who has a cold.
s.m. A monkey, an ape (syn. bandar); — an epithet of Vishṇu; — a pulley; a block
subs: or kapi-śreshṭha, or kapīs or kapīś (˚pi+īś˚), or kopīndra (˚pi+in˚), s.m. 'Lord or chief of the monkeys,' an epithet of Vishṇu
s.m. An epithet of the son of Bāli, king of the monkeys.

def: adj. (f. -ā), 'Monkey-coloured,' brown, tawny, reddish; — s.m. Name of a Hindū sage, the founder of the Sānkhya system of philosophy.
s.f. A brown or reddish-coloured cow.
s.m. = kapīndra, q.v.s.v. کپي kapi.
adj. & s.m. 'Ape-coloured,' brown, reddish brown; — brown or reddish colour.
s.m. The wood-apple tree, Feronia elephantum; the wood-apple ( = kaith).
s.f. = kap-kapī, q.v.
s.f. See s.v. kap.
v.n. = کانپنا kāṅpnā, q.v.
v.n. To tremble, shake, &c. ( = kāṅpnā, q.v.); — adj. (f. -ī), Trembling, tremulous.
s.m. The cheek
subs: s.m. A small round pillow to rest the cheek upon.

def: s.m. (f. -ī), A dove; a pigeon; the spotted-necked pigeon.
s.m. (dialec.), See kapaṛ and kapṛā.

ety: Prk. कप्पडो, and कप्पडओ; S. कर्पटः and कर्पट+कः
def: s.m. Cloth; clothing, clothes, dress, habit
subs: or kapṛoṅ-kī pot, s.f. A bundle of clothes
or kapaṛ-dhūl, s.m. Tissue; gauze; crape (worn as a sāṛī); a silk cloth wrought with gold and silver flowers (worn as a head-dress by women)
or kapaṛ-ćhan, adj. Thoroughly strained or sifted; impalpable (powder); deep (consultation)
(or -ćhan) karnā, v.t. To strain, to sift
(S. karpaṭa+kuṭta), s.m. lit. 'Pounded up with rags'; clay made for crucibles, &c.
or kapṛā pahannā, To put on a covering or a garment, to cover or clothe oneself
s.m. Clothes, clothing, articles of apparel
s.m. pl. Clothes, clothing, dress, apparel
(-ko), or kapṛoṅ-se honā, or kapṛoṅ honā, v.n. To have the menses
To put on clothes, to dress
To dye cloth or clothes; to become a faqīr
To give an alarm (by making the clothes bulge out)

def: s.f. The smell of burnt cloth or cotton.
s.f. Straining through cloth, sifting; covering the mouth of a vessel with cloth
subs: v.t. To cover the mouth of a vessel with cloth; to strain through cloth, to sift.

def: s.m. Captain.
s.f. Captaincy, office of a captain
s.m.(?) A gate, door (see qābū).
s.m. = کافور kāfūr, q.v.
s.m. Camphor; — name of a flower
subs: s.f. The root of the fragrant grass Hedychium spicatum.

def: s.m. Testicles (of a buffalo, or ram, or he-goat, or black buck, &c.).
adj. As white, or as fragrant, as camphor; — (kapūr+S. इका), s.f. Betel-leaf (pān) of a yellowish colour.
s.m. A mean contemptible man, a vile wretch; a coward.
s.f. Undutifulness (in a son); degeneracy.

ety: v.n. fr. قعر 'to go to the bottom'; 'to make deep,' &c.
def: s.m. Bottom, or depth (of a well, &c.); a hollow, cavity, low ground; a gulf, an abyss
subs: s.m. Depth of a river; trough of the sea; the sea

ety: old P. kāra; Zend kāra, rt. kar; S. कार, rt. कृ
def: s.m. Act, action; work, operation, labour, occupation, service, business, function, duty, profession; affair, matter; use, practice
subs: (for kār-āmada), adj. Come into use; useful, serviceable; available, applicable; conducive (to); — kār-āmad karnā, v.t. To make available, or of use, &c.
vulg. kār-āmdanī, and kār-āmada (vulg. -āmda), adj. = kār-āmad, q.v.
or kār-o-bār, s.m. Work, labour, business; affairs; dealings, transactions
s.m. One engaged in business; a trader; a transactor of business, a man of business; a manager, an officer
s.f. Undertaking a work or business
adj. Giving attention (to, -kā, any business), acting (upon); proceeding; obedient
(-kā), To give attention (to); to act (upon or up to), to comply (with), to perform or discharge (a duty); to obey
s.m. A transactor of business; one who manages or carries on a business, a manager, an agent; a negotiator; a factor; an officer, or employé
s.f. Completion of business; management of business; management, agency
s.m. Embroidery; — an embroiderer; — a frame for embroidering
adj. Embroidered; — s.f. Embroidery, working of flowers and leaves, &c. on cloth with gold and silver thread; cloth so embroidered

s.m. A workshop, factory, manufactory; an arsenal; a dockyard; a laboratory; any place where public works are carried on; an office; a great work; a business, concern; way of action, procedure; — a lace-machine; — vulva
s.m. pl. (of kār-ḵẖāna, q.v.), Workshops, factories, &c.
s.m. Proprietor of a factory, &c.; — a steward, a butler
s.f. The business of a workshop or factory, &c.
To open a workshop or business, &c.
adj. & s.m. Experienced in business, skilled, skilful, expert, versed; — a skilful or expert worker, an adept, a connoiseur
s.f. Knowledge of business, experience, skill, expertness

s.f. Usefulness; working, operation; carrying on of a business; occupation, employment; management, conduct, execution, performance; process, proceeding, procedure; — kār-rawāʼī karnā (-kī), To execute or discharge a function or duty; to hold proceedings
adj. & s.m. Dexterous, adroit, skilful, clever; — a doer; maker; the Maker, the Deity
s.f. Work; organization; mechanism; adjustment, order; — cleverness, skill, adroitness, dexterity; artfulness, artifice, cheating, dodge
s.m. Place of work; seat of action
adj. Conversant or skilful in business; experienced
s.m. An emperor; a minister; a commander; a superintendent; anyone vested with power
s.f. Execution; administration; government, rule, authority
adj. Experienced in business, experienced, trained, &c. ( = kār-āzmūda, q.v.)
s.m. A director, manager; an officer whose business it is to keep records; an assignee; a manager of a minor's estate; a representative; an attorney
or kār-gah, s.f. A place for work; a workshop, &c. ( = kār-ḵẖāna)
adj. & s.m. Skilful in business, expert, expeditious; industrious; attentive, dutiful; — a skilful, or an expeditious, worker, &c.; a manager, factor; — a curator
s.f. Despatch of business; discharge of duty; management, expertness, skill; — employment, service; good service

adj. & s.m. Doing the work; performing the deed; accomplishing (one's) purpose; successful; efficient, effective, efficacious, of avail; serviceable, useful operative, active (as a medicine); moving, stirring, telling (as an oration, &c.); penetrating (as a sword); — one who labours in adjusting affairs, a skilful negotiator; an intelligent man of business; — a workman ( = kārī-gar)
s.m. A record of stirring deeds, an account of battles, military history; chronicle, history; memoirs; a commentary

def: s.m. 'Who or what does or makes or causes'; a father; — the hand; the trunk of an elephant
subs: s.m. A kind of small cymbal
s.f. Beating time by clapping the hands
s.m. The palm of the hand
vulg. karaj, s.m. lit. 'Produced in or from the hand'; a finger; a finger-nail
To join the hands supplicatingly before one, to beg, entreat
or kar-joṛī, s.f. Having the hands placed flat together (as in supplication)
s.m. Odematous swelling of the hand or hands
(S. grahaṇa), s.m. 'Taking the hand'; protection; marriage
s.f. The hands used as a rosary, the finger-joints corresponding to the beads
adj. With the hands clasped (as in earnest entreaty).

def: s.m. A ray of light, sunbeam, moonbeam; hail; royal revenue, revenue from taxes, tax, tribute; toll, fee, duty, custom; a settled allowance to village officers and servants
subs: or kar-ughāʼū, s.m. Collector of revenue, tax-gatherer, toll-collector
or kar lagānā (-par), To impose a tax (on), to tax
s.f. Imposing a cess or tax, taxation.

ety: old H. करि; Prk. करे or करिआ; S. कृत्वा, rt. कृ
def: part. (of karnā), Having done, &c.; — adv. & postpn. As, like, after the manner of; by the name or title of; by reason of, in consequence of, on account of; in respect of, as regards, as to; by means of, by, with, through (the conj. part. kar prefixed to certain verbs serves to form a class of intensives, or completives, &c.; see s.v. karnā)
subs: or kar-ke, or kar-kar-kar, or kar-kar-ke (by redupl. of kar; see ke), part., adv. & postpn. = kar (kar-ke or kar-kar is the more common form. Joined to substantives and certain adjectives it forms adverbs, e.g. kram-karke, 'respectively,' 'in order' ( = kram-se), 'gradually'; — dhruva-karke, 'firmly;' — mukhya-karke, 'principally.' Sometimes it is annexed to adverbs to emphasize them, e.g. avaśya-kar or kar-ke, 'necessarily, &c.' It is sometimes used as synonymous with se, e.g. ādhīntāʼī-karke = adhīntāʼī-se; — jal-karke snān, 'bathing with water'; — jis-karke ( = jis-se), 'by whom'; — us-karke ( = us-se), 'by him'; (see also kram-karke, above). Phrases in which kar-ke occurs are numerous, e.g. ek-ek-karke, 'one after another,' 'one at a time'; — būṅd-būṅd-karke, 'drop by drop,' 'a drop at a time'; — bahut-karke, 'in great measure,' 'at the most'; — viśesh-karke, 'particularly,' 'especially'; — satya-karke, 'in fact,' 'verily').

def: postpn. of the gen. (dialec. and rustic) = kā, q.v.
s.f. The loins, the hip and loins, the waist
subs: s.m. A string tied round the waist, to which a waist-cloth (langoṭī) is fastened (cf. kar-dhanī; kar-gadā).

def: adj. Deaf; — s.m. Purpose, intention, will, desire; power, strength; grandeur, pomp; — felicity
subs: or karr-o-farr, s.m. Pomp and show or pride, parade.

def: s.f. The seed of safflower, Carthamus tinctorius.
adj. & s.m. = kālā, q.v.
s.f. A prison (not generally used alone in Hindī, but in comp.)
subs: (˚rā+āg˚), s.m. A place of confinement, a prison, gaol

def: s.m. = kāṛhā, q.v.
dialec. postpn. of the gen. (f. -ī) = kā, q.v.

ety: Prk. करिअओ, or कलिअओ; S. कर (fr. कृ)+इत+कः
def: m. (f. -ī), The old (and regular) perfect part. of the verb karnā, 'to do,' &c. ( = kiyā).

s.m. A ring; a massive ring of gold or silver, &c. (worn on the wrists, or ankles), a bangle, bracelet; anklet; link (of a chain); handle (of a door); hilt (of a sword); anything resembling a ring or bracelet in form; — a large thick beam or rafter (see kaṛī); a kind of sweetmeat; — a verse or line (of Hindī poetry).
adj. (f. -ī), Hard, firm, stiff, &c. ( = kaṛā, q.v.); — adulterated; false, bad (as coin).

ety: S. कर्करः; or Prk. कड्डओ = S. कष्ट+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Hard, stiff, tight, firm, rigid; tough; strong, powerful; hardy, vigorous; firm, resolute, unbending, obdurate; harsh, stern, severe, austere, morose; violent, vehement; rigorous; difficult, painful, grievous, distressing; scarce; dear, high-priced; — arch, sly, knowing, penetrating; — kaṛā karnā, v.t. To harden, to make tough; to stiffen, to tighten; to make strong (as tea, wine, &c.); to raise (the price of a thing); to embolden, encourage, &c.
subs: or kaṛī jhelnā, v.n. To suffer distress or hardship, &c.
or kaṛī kahnā (-ko), To use harsh or severe language (to), to rate soundly.

def: s.m. A young buffalo.

ety: prob. fr. A. قربة qirbat, fr. قرب; see qarābat
def: s.m. A flagon; a large glass bottle; a carboy (cf. Fr. carafe).
s.f. Compound medicines; antidotes; a medicine; an antidote; — pharmacopœia.

ety: for A. قرابة, inf. n. of قرب 'to be or become near'
def: s.f. Nearness, vicinity, propinquity; affinity, connection, relation, kindred, kin, consanguinity, alliance
subs: s.m. A relation by blood or marriage, a relative, kinsman, a family connexion

def: adj. & s.m. Related (to), connected, of kin, akin (to), consanguineous; — a relative, relation, &c. ( = qarābat-dār, q.v.).
s.f. Compound medicines; antidotes; a medicine; an antidote; — pharmacopœia.
s.f. = H کربير करबीर karbīr, s.m. = kara-vīr, q.v.
s.f. A carbine.
s.m. The oval-leafed rosebay, Nerium antidysentericum (the seeds of which are used in medicine as a tonic or febrifuge, syn. indar-jau); also N. tinctorum, or Wrightia tinctoria.
s.f. Parsley
s.m. A shallow wide-mouthed iron boiler (for boiling sugar in, &c.), a boiling-pan, boiler, cauldron (also written kaṛhāyā)
subs: s.m., or kaṛāhe-kī pūjā, s.f. A ceremony which consists in worshipping the boiling-pan when put on the fire for the first time on some festive occasion.

def: s.m. A moan, a groan.
s.m. = kaṛāhā, q.v.
s.m. A shallow wide-mouthed iron boiler (for boiling sugar in, &c.), a boiling-pan, boiler, cauldron (also written kaṛhāyā)
subs: s.m., or kaṛāhe-kī pūjā, s.f. A ceremony which consists in worshipping the boiling-pan when put on the fire for the first time on some festive occasion.

def: s.m. A camel.
s.m. (f. ?), The loins, the waist; the buttocks.

ety: for A. کراهة, inf. n. of کره 'to abhor,' &c.
def: s.f. Aversion, dislike, disgust, loathing, horror, abhorrence, detestation.

ety: for A. کراهةً, acc. of karāhat
def: adv. Unwillingly, reluctantly, with dislike or disgust.
s.f. = kaṛāhī, q.v.
s.f. A small boiling-pan; a frying-pan; — anything cooked in a kaṛāhī (such as, kaćaurī, pūrī, ḥalwā, &c.); an offering of sweetmeats
subs: To cast a spell over a frying-pan

To lick the pot (it is a superstition among Indian women that licking the pot brings about the ill-luck of a fall of rain on the wedding procession of the person given to this habit; hence, on the occurrence of such a contretemps the women abuse the bridegroom for having licked the pot)
The boiling-pan to be put on the fire; preparation to be made (for a feast)
(-ko), To offer sweetmeats, cakes, &c. (to a goddess)

To put the hand in a pan of boiling oil as an ordeal (an accused person is required to take out a piece of gold which has been dropped in a pan of boiling oil; if he can do this without being scalded, he is held innocent).

def: s.f. A dish consisting of the meal of pulse (chick pea or Cicer arietinum) dressed with spices and sour milk (dahī).

ety: for A. کراهية, inf. n. of کره
def: s.f. Aversion, &c. ( = karāhat, q.v.); disagreeableness; abominable act, abomination; any infraction of moral or religions obligation (as eating or drinking impure or prohibited food, wearing obnoxious attire, committing acts of indecency, &c.)
subs: (-ko), To regard with aversion, &c.; to contemn, scorn

ety: karāh˚, prob. = S. ल्किश्य(ते), rt. ल्किश्
def: v.n. To grieve, mourn, repine; to sigh, to utter 'ah'; to moan, to groan.
s.m. A granary, storehouse.
s.f. A small earthen pipkin.
s.f. = kaṛāhī, q.v.

ety: kaṛā, q.v.+āʼī˚ = Prk. अइआ = S. ता+इका
def: s.f. Hardness, firmness, stiffness, toughness; obduracy; severity, rigour, &c. (see kaṛā)
subs: To practise severity, &c.; to be severe; to be austere; to be firm or resolute; to harden oneself; to be obdurate, &c.

def: s.m. pl. (of qarīna, q.v.), Connections; conditions; circumstances; presumptions; conjectures; — the context.

ety: kārā = kālā, q.v. + aʼit = Prk. आयंतो = S. आपयन्; see ḍhalait
def: s.m. Name of a snake (of a very venomous kind).

ety: kārā = kālā, q.v. + aʼit = Prk. आयंतो = S. आपयन्; see ḍhalait
def: s.m. Name of a snake (of a very venomous kind).
s.m. See s.v. kar, 'hand.'

adj. & s.m. (used as last member of compounds, — f. -ikā), Doing, acting; making, causing, producing; — a doer; an agent; one who effects or produces anything; — s.m. Action (esp. in Gram.); — case (of a noun)
subs: s.f. (in Gram.) The formation of cases, declension.
